slides.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
slides.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
slides.speaker_notes: Export kind: policy_contract_comparison_deck. Deck family: comparison. Sources: structured_result, document_agent_comparison.

slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
The Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) is a structured register that enforces strict provenance and archival requirements for vendor control evidence, directly impacting control closure timelines and compliance posture through status tracking (Received, Missing, In progress). In contrast, the Privileged Account Approval Email outlines a conditional, time-bound access approval contingent on strict usage controls, logging compliance, quarterly review, and vendor confirmation, with revalidation triggered by personnel changes.  

- EVD-101 is a synthetic, structured register for tracking evidence validity; the approval email is a conditional governance directive tied to specific operational controls.  
- EVD-101 requires evidence to be verified and archived to close controls; the approval email mandates logging, recertification, and vendor oversight for ongoing access.  
- EVD-101 status tracking (Received, Missing, In progress) directly determines control closure; the approval email requires quarterly review and revalidation upon personnel changes.  
- EVD-101 enforces archival provenance for evidence; the approval email emphasizes accountability through usage logging and vendor confirmation.  
- EVD-101 is a Q1 2024 compliance tool for evidence management; the approval email is a standing governance mechanism for privileged access.  
- EVD-101 blocks control closure until evidence is confirmed; the approval email requires revalidation if personnel change, not evidence gaps.
